description:
You are an urban planner at the San Francisco Planning Department preparing a housing affordability briefing for city council. Using the San Francisco UrbanSim dataset at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, perform a zone-level housing affordability analysis. For each zone with at least 10 households, calculate the median household income, median residential sales price (for buildings with price > 0), price-to-income ratio, and total household count. Identify the 5 most affordable and 5 least affordable zones based on the price-to-income ratio.

Document the analysis in a Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/affordability_analysis.ipynb with all cell outputs visible. Export the full results for qualifying zones to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/affordability_by_zone.csv (including columns: zone_id, median_income, median_price, price_to_income_ratio, num_households). Generate a bar chart showing the price-to-income ratio for all qualifying zones, sorted ascending, and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/affordability_chart.png.
